items.headline: Tom Dreesen, Sinatra’s longtime opening act, dies at 86
items.dek: The Chicago stand-up and one half of the pioneering duo Tim & Tom died June 17 at 86.
items.synopsis: Tom Dreesen, the warm, sharp Chicago stand-up who spent thirteen years as Frank Sinatra’s opening act, died June 17 at 86. He broke in during the late 1960s alongside Tim Reid as Tim & Tom, one of America’s first interracial comedy duos, then built a five-decade solo career of more than 500 national TV spots, including over 60 on “The Tonight Show” and frequent turns guest-hosting Letterman. A consummate opening act, he warmed up crowds for Sammy Davis Jr., Liza Minnelli and Smokey Robinson before Sinatra made him his regular. His final TV appearance came just days earlier, on June 9.

items.headline: John Mulaney becomes the first comedian to sell out Wrigley Field
items.dek: Mulaney sold out the 40,000-seat ballpark for a July 11 show on his “Mister Whatever” tour.
items.synopsis: John Mulaney has become the first comedian to sell out Wrigley Field, the 40,000-plus-seat home of the Chicago Cubs, Deadline reported June 17. The Chicago native — who “called his shot” last fall as the first comic to headline the ballpark — now joins a short list of artists to fill it that includes Paul McCartney, Bruce Springsteen and Pearl Jam. The July 11 date is part of his “Mister Whatever” tour. “I’m honored to stand on the same field where I saw Mark Grace rip cigarettes during games, and yell all of my jokes,” he told Deadline.

items.headline: Dan Soder tapes his first full Netflix hour in Mill Valley
items.dek: The “Billions” actor and stand-up filmed his debut Netflix special June 13 at the Throckmorton Theatre.
items.synopsis: Dan Soder taped his first full-length Netflix special on June 13 at the 142 Throckmorton Theatre in Mill Valley, California. The hour marks his return to the streamer after a breakout half-hour on “The Standups” back in 2017, and it lands in the middle of his 25-city “The Golden Retriever of Comedy” tour, now on its second leg. Soder is also known for seven seasons as Mafee on Showtime’s “Billions” and for hosting the “Soder” podcast. A release date has not yet been set.

items.headline: Josh Johnson launches his “Comedy Band Camp” tour in Ottawa
items.dek: The Daily Show writer opened his 34-city stand-up-and-live-music tour June 13 at the National Arts Centre.
items.synopsis: Josh Johnson kicked off his “Comedy Band Camp” tour June 12–13 at Southam Hall in Ottawa’s National Arts Centre, a 34-city run stretching through the end of the year. The show fuses his stand-up with live music, trading the usual club set for something closer to a summer-camp jam session. A “Daily Show” correspondent whom WIRED dubbed “the funniest guy on the internet,” Johnson has built an outsized following by posting weekly stand-up online. The tour continues across North America into December.

items.headline: Leslie Jones drops her hour “Life Part 2” free on YouTube
items.dek: The former SNL star posted her full special, filmed at L.A.’s Belasco, free to YouTube on June 14.
items.synopsis: Leslie Jones released her stand-up hour “Life Part 2” free on her own YouTube channel on June 14, following its run on Peacock. Filmed before a live audience at the Belasco in downtown Los Angeles, the 59-minute set gives the former “Saturday Night Live” force room to stretch out on aging, fame, relationships, therapy and her long search for a soulmate. In one stretch she stages the celebrity-packed funeral she’d want for herself, refusing as ever to leave the production to anyone else. The free upload puts the special in front of the widest possible audience.

items.headline: Kevin Hart’s Hartbeat sets 12 new “LOL Live” specials at Hulu
items.dek: Hartbeat’s half-hour stand-up showcase returns to Hulu with 12 specials, starting June 19 with Chaunté Wayans.
items.synopsis: Hartbeat, the studio Kevin Hart co-founded, is bringing its “LOL Live” showcase back to Hulu with a dozen new half-hour stand-up specials, Deadline reported June 17. The slate opens June 19 with Chaunté Wayans and rolls out one a month through May 2027, spotlighting talent including Dulcé Sloan, Tacarra Williams and Comedian CP. Hart executive produces, and the franchise is pitched as a deliberate revival of the half-hour special, long a proving ground for rising comics. The episodes were taped at WePlay Studios in Inglewood.

items.headline: Underbelly unveils a 185-show Edinburgh Fringe 2026 program
items.dek: The Fringe giant’s June 18 lineup is led by Sara Pascoe, Simon Amstell, Russell Kane and Rory Bremner.
items.synopsis: Underbelly revealed its 2026 Edinburgh Festival Fringe program on June 18: 185 shows spread across Bristo Square, Cowgate, George Square and the Circus Hub on the Meadows. The comedy bill is led by household names making one- and two-night stands, including Sara Pascoe with “Sara Pascoe: For One Night Only” at McEwan Hall and Simon Amstell bringing “I Love It Here” after a sold-out London run. Russell Kane returns with “HyperActive” sixteen years after winning an Edinburgh Comedy Award, alongside Rory Bremner and Nina Conti. The Fringe runs August 7–31.
